pattern: Membership testing against a set of jewel types turns the count into a single linear scan over the stones.
hints: You repeatedly ask whether a stone character is one of the jewel characters, so make that question fast.
Put every jewel character into a hash set first, then each stone is an O(1) membership test.
Build a set from jewels, then count the stones whose character is in that set; the total is the answer.

edge_inputs.description: mixed case where lower and upper count separately
case sensitivity means a jewel of wrong case matches nothing
single jewel and single stone that match
single jewel and single stone that differ
jewels listed but absent from the stones

stress.description: random jewel set and a long stone string drawn from the full letter alphabet
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    import string
    letters = string.ascii_letters
    jcount = rng.randint(1, min(50, len(letters)))
    jewels = ''.join(rng.sample(letters, jcount))
    slen = rng.randint(1, 50)
    stones = ''.join(rng.choice(letters) for _ in range(slen))
    return [jewels, stones]
stress.note: jewels are sampled without replacement to keep them unique per the constraint
